message:
fix(net/tcp/split): register combined waiter interest on re-registration

When TcpStreamInner falls through to fresh driver.register(), it
previously passed only the triggering direction's interest (e.g.,
READABLE when the read half hit WouldBlock).  If both halves had
stored wakers, the write half's interest was silently dropped from
the registration, causing lost wakeups.

Add registration_interest() helper that unions the interests of all
stored waiters and falls back to the triggering interest only when
no waiters are present.  Apply it in the re-registration path so the
epoll/kqueue fd watches both directions.

- New inline fn registration_interest(read_waiter, write_waiter, fallback)
- Apply in TcpStreamInner::register_interest after building the
  combined waker, before dropping the guard
- New test: registration_interest_prefers_waiter_union verifying
  both-waiters, single-waiter, and no-waiters-fallback cases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
